action.content:
You are playing a text adventure game. I will describe what you can perceive in the game. You write the single action you want to take in the game starting with >. Only reply with an action.
For example:
> open cupboard

You can have up to two objects in your inventory at the same time.

Your goal for this game is to brew a potion following the potion recipe.

Once you have achieved your goal, write "> done" to end the game.

You are in a bedroom now. There are a bed, a book, a wardrobe, a pillow and a potion recipe. The book is on the bed. The wardrobe is closed. The pillow is on the floor. The potion recipe is on the bed. There is a passage to a reading room here.
action.label: context

action.content: You take the potion recipe. In your inventory you have a potion recipe.
action.label: context

action.content: You are in a reading room now. There are a side table, a couch, a chair, a table and a shelf. There are passages to a garden and a conservatory here.
action.label: context

action.content: This is a shelf. There is nothing on the shelf.
action.label: context

action.label: context

action.content:
You are in a magic workshop now. There are a fire wand, a zulpowand, a ice wand, a orrery, a workbench and a fairy wand. The fire wand is on the ceiling. The zulpowand is on the ceiling. The ice wand is on the ceiling. The fairy wand is on the ceiling. There are passages to a kitchen and a bedroom here.
Magical experiments have reversed and dampened gravity in this room. You begin to float in the air.
